text:
Once upon a quaint English village lived a lady named Amelia. She loved reading old fairy tales and always dreamed of visiting ancient castles. Amelia rarely left her cozy study strewn with volumes on folklore and folklore animals. One day, while rummaging through a vintage book shop, she discovered a dusty tome with illustrations of majestic dragons. Fascinated, Amelia read about these fire-breathing creatures that could shape their own feathers into any kind of wing.

Inspired, Amelia spent weeks poring over the book, sketching dragon wings. She painted intricate patterns on canvas, dreaming of a dragon fluttering around her room. Amelia's favorite bookworm perch became a majestic dragon perched on a branch waving farewell with one feathered wing.

Every afternoon after her reading session, Ameliawould sit by her window dreaming about her dragon friend. One day, a peculiar old man visited the village market. He didn’t speak much, but his small wooden box of extraordinary feathers caught Amelia's eye.

The old man
